title: Finding the union and intersection of two sets
problem:
Sets $A = \{1, 3, 5, 7, 9\}$ and $B = \{5, 7, 9, 11, 13\}$.

(a) List $A \cap B$ and state $|A \cap B|$.
(b) List $A \cup B$ and state $|A \cup B|$.

steps.instruction: Identify $A \cap B$ by listing elements that appear in both sets.
Find $A \cup B$ by listing all distinct elements from either set.
steps.working:
$$A \cup B = \{1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13\}$$
Elements $5$, $7$ and $9$ are shared and counted once only.
$$|A \cup B| = 7$$

title: Applying the inclusion-exclusion principle
problem: In a year group of 80 students, 48 study Drama and 35 study Music. If 12 students study both subjects, how many students study neither?

steps.instruction: State the inclusion-exclusion principle for two sets.
Substitute the known values to find $|D \cup M|$.
steps.working:
$$|D \cup M| = 48 + 35 - 12 = 71$$
Seventy-one students study at least one of the two subjects.

steps.instruction: Subtract from the total to find students who study neither.

answer: 9 students study neither Drama nor Music.

title: Solving a three-set Venn diagram problem
problem:
In a group of 60 people, 32 like coffee, 28 like tea and 20 like juice. 14 like coffee and tea, 10 like coffee and juice, 8 like tea and juice, and 4 like all three beverages. Find:

(a) the number who like exactly one beverage
(b) the number who like none of the three beverages.

steps.instruction: Find the number who like only coffee by subtracting all overlaps and adding back those counted twice.
Apply the same formula for only tea and only juice.
Sum the three 'only' regions to answer part (a).
Apply the three-set inclusion-exclusion formula to find $|C \cup T \cup J|$, then subtract from 60 for part (b).

answer: (a) 28 people like exactly one beverage. (b) 8 people like none of the three beverages.
